text.content:
Cooking

Characters with Cook's Utensils (or Brewer's Supplies / Herbalism Kit) and the matching tool proficiency can prepare meals during a rest. Cooking is a camp activity. It takes the character's activity slot for the night.

How Cooking Works

  1. Select a recipe from the recipe browser.
  2. Choose an approach: Standard (base DC) or Ambitious (DC +5, superior output on success).
  3. Roll a Survival check against the recipe's DC. Ingredients are consumed regardless of the outcome.

Party Meals (Feasts)

Some recipes are party meals: a single crafted item that feeds and buffs the entire party. These are marked with a party icon in the recipe browser.

Party meals have scaled ingredients: certain ingredients (typically rations) cost more based on party size. The formula is max(1, partySize - 2) per scaled ingredient. The fixed ingredients (the hero ingredient and water) stay the same regardless of party size.

Example: Hearty Stew

PartyFresh MeatRationsWaterskinTotal
31113
41214
51315
61416
71517

The pot needs its foundation: a chunk of meat for flavour and a skin of water for the broth. After that, each person adds a ration to the pot.

Well Fed

Eating a cooked meal (or being served a feast) grants the Well Fed condition. While Well Fed, a character gains temporary hit points based on the cook's proficiency bonus. The formula is (PB-1)d6 for standard meals, stepping up one die size for ambitious successes.

Recipe Authoring (Content Packs)

Content packs can define custom recipes. To mark an ingredient as scaling with party size, add perPartyMember: true to the ingredient definition:

{ "name": "Rations", "quantity": 1, "perPartyMember": true }

This flag only takes effect when the recipe has partyMeal: true in its output flags. Non-party-meal recipes ignore the flag.
